intro: Arctopoa alexejii, also known as Russian Thyme or Alpine Thyme, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Apiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Arctopoa alexejii include thymol, carvacrol, and borneol, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Arctopoa alexejii include tea, infusions, and topical creams, which are often used to soothe coughs, calm digestive issues, and promote wound healing.
